builder: Claude Code S4_BUILD executor, session to be selected before S4
builder_boundary: External builder may only own S4_BUILD after S3 implementation plan; it may not edit spec, verification plan, verifier, release, or push authority files.

verify_arch.reason: Spec declares multiple surfaces: skills, hooks/API, local orch handoff, tests, file artifacts, message disclosure.

acceptance.then: use superpower enters visible FSM before ad hoc execution
two-pass design loop cannot skip SOTA before final spec
verification-plan/test-design is required before implementation plan
Codex guidance avoids nonexistent Skill callable while Claude keeps Skill tool
active writing-arch references are absent or historical only
Claude builder can own implementation only after approved plan
prompt and gate behavior has deterministic regression coverage
substantial specs produce review artifacts or explicit N/A reason
all user-raised issues have durable multidimensional elicitation coverage
pause and transition responses include compact progress line

cases.given: SFSM-01 is evaluated from the approved Capability Registry, not from the implementation plan
cases.when: the verifier exercises the happy condition for using-superpowers skill
cases.then: the nominal path delivers the declared outcome: use superpower enters visible FSM before ad hoc execution

cases.given: SFSM-01 is evaluated from the approved Capability Registry, not from the implementation plan
cases.when: the verifier exercises the boundary condition for using-superpowers skill
cases.then: the smallest valid prompt and the full remaining gate list still deliver the declared outcome: use superpower enters visible FSM before ad hoc execution

cases.given: SFSM-01 is evaluated from the approved Capability Registry, not from the implementation plan
cases.when: the verifier exercises the negative condition for using-superpowers skill
cases.then: premature or incomplete action is blocked instead of silently advancing: use superpower enters visible FSM before ad hoc execution

cases.given: SFSM-01 is evaluated from the approved Capability Registry, not from the implementation plan
cases.when: the verifier exercises the integration condition for using-superpowers skill
cases.then: the declared surface and adjacent hook/artifact cooperate to deliver the outcome: use superpower enters visible FSM before ad hoc execution

cases.given: SFSM-01 is evaluated from the approved Capability Registry, not from the implementation plan
cases.when: the verifier exercises the corner condition for using-superpowers skill
cases.then: combined runtime, delegation, and gate-state variations preserve the outcome: use superpower enters visible FSM before ad hoc execution

cases.given: SFSM-01 is evaluated from the approved Capability Registry, not from the implementation plan
cases.when: the verifier exercises the error-guessing condition for using-superpowers skill
cases.then: a known failure pattern is targeted and caught by the guard: use superpower enters visible FSM before ad hoc execution

cases.given: SFSM-01 is evaluated from the approved Capability Registry, not from the implementation plan
cases.when: an unscripted exploratory charter for using-superpowers skill: probing remaining_flow/compact_progress with gate sequences outside the scripted happy/boundary/negative inputs (a conditional gate like S5_FIX_LOOP, or an unrecognized gate string) to find behavior the scripted cases never anticipated
cases.then: the module either handles the unscripted input sanely with a clear, documented result, or the gap is recorded as a defect — it is not silently assumed covered by the scripted cases

cases.given: SFSM-01 is evaluated from the approved Capability Registry, not from the implementation plan
cases.when: a forbidden-state oracle for using-superpowers skill is checked directly, independent of any single scripted example
cases.then: an already-passed gate must NEVER reappear in the remaining-flow list, and next= must NEVER be empty except at the terminal gate

cases.given: SFSM-02 is evaluated from the approved Capability Registry, not from the implementation plan
cases.when: an unscripted exploratory charter for R-PIPE spec gate: attempting to reach S1_SPEC_FINAL via out-of-order or partial artifact injection (skipping S1_SOTA/S1_REVISE_DISCUSS) that is not one of the literal scripted inputs
cases.then: the module either handles the unscripted input sanely with a clear, documented result, or the gap is recorded as a defect — it is not silently assumed covered by the scripted cases

cases.given: SFSM-02 is evaluated from the approved Capability Registry, not from the implementation plan
cases.when: a forbidden-state oracle for R-PIPE spec gate is checked directly, independent of any single scripted example
cases.then: a spec must NEVER be permitted to progress while its SYS.1 material_unknowns still has an unresolved entry, cross-checked against the FSM gate independently of the elicitation gate

cases.given: SFSM-03 is evaluated from the approved Capability Registry, not from the implementation plan
cases.when: an unscripted exploratory charter for R-PIPE plan gate: feeding validate_gate_prerequisites artifact-dict shapes not enumerated in the scripted cases (truthy-but-malformed test_design, flapping test_design_clean) to check it does not silently accept a partial artifact
cases.then: the module either handles the unscripted input sanely with a clear, documented result, or the gap is recorded as a defect — it is not silently assumed covered by the scripted cases

cases.given: SFSM-03 is evaluated from the approved Capability Registry, not from the implementation plan
cases.when: a forbidden-state oracle for R-PIPE plan gate is checked directly, independent of any single scripted example
cases.then: S4_BUILD must NEVER be reachable while test_design_clean is False, regardless of how many other artifacts are true

cases.given: SFSM-04 is evaluated from the approved Capability Registry, not from the implementation plan
cases.when: an unscripted exploratory charter for runtime skill surface hooks: feeding validate_skill_invocation_guidance near-miss phrasings not in the scripted fixtures ("Skill (" with a space, "the Skill tool", mixed-case "SKILL()", a code-fenced Skill()) to probe whether detection is regex-narrow and misses obvious near-misses
cases.then: the module either handles the unscripted input sanely with a clear, documented result, or the gap is recorded as a defect — it is not silently assumed covered by the scripted cases

cases.given: SFSM-04 is evaluated from the approved Capability Registry, not from the implementation plan
cases.when: a forbidden-state oracle for runtime skill surface hooks is checked directly, independent of any single scripted example
cases.then: invocation_contract("codex") must NEVER contain the literal substring "Skill(" under any future edit, asserted as an invariant on the current contract text

cases.given: SFSM-05 is evaluated from the approved Capability Registry, not from the implementation plan
cases.when: an unscripted exploratory charter for fork maintenance docs: an unscripted repo-wide search for "writing-arch" beyond the harness's fixed path list (skills/hooks/lib/tests/docs/FORK-MAINTENANCE.md) to check whether drift could hide in an unscanned file (README, CHANGELOG, root-level docs)
cases.then: the module either handles the unscripted input sanely with a clear, documented result, or the gap is recorded as a defect — it is not silently assumed covered by the scripted cases

cases.given: SFSM-05 is evaluated from the approved Capability Registry, not from the implementation plan
cases.when: a forbidden-state oracle for fork maintenance docs is checked directly, independent of any single scripted example
cases.then: no file in the repository may contain an ACTIVE (non-historical/superseded/obsolete) writing-arch reference, asserted across the whole tree rather than the harness's fixed scan set

cases.given: SFSM-06 is evaluated from the approved Capability Registry, not from the implementation plan
cases.when: an unscripted exploratory charter for local orch job/outbox: fuzzing owner strings not in the scripted example ("claude:" lowercase, "CLAUDE:x", "builder-ish:y", empty string, whitespace-only) to check the prefix match neither over- nor under-triggers
cases.then: the module either handles the unscripted input sanely with a clear, documented result, or the gap is recorded as a defect — it is not silently assumed covered by the scripted cases

cases.given: SFSM-06 is evaluated from the approved Capability Registry, not from the implementation plan
cases.when: a forbidden-state oracle for local orch job/outbox is checked directly, independent of any single scripted example
cases.then: an external builder (Claude:* or builder:* prefix) must NEVER validate clean for any gate other than S4_BUILD, asserted across the full STAGES list rather than the 5 gates in the existing negative test

cases.given: SFSM-07 is evaluated from the approved Capability Registry, not from the implementation plan
cases.when: an unscripted exploratory charter for tests: running the regression harness against a deliberately corrupted environment (missing test-design.md, or test-design.json with no registry) to confirm it fails loudly rather than silently exiting 0
cases.then: the module either handles the unscripted input sanely with a clear, documented result, or the gap is recorded as a defect — it is not silently assumed covered by the scripted cases

cases.given: SFSM-07 is evaluated from the approved Capability Registry, not from the implementation plan
cases.when: a forbidden-state oracle for tests is checked directly, independent of any single scripted example
cases.then: the regression harness must NEVER exit 0 while the S2 verify artifacts are git-dirty, asserted as an explicit invariant alongside the script's own step-4 check

cases.given: SFSM-08 is evaluated from the approved Capability Registry, not from the implementation plan
cases.when: an unscripted exploratory charter for visual review artifacts: feeding validate_visual_artifact_policy spec_meta combinations not in the scripted fixtures (unrecognized spec_type, a mix of one valid and one malformed visual_artifacts entry) to probe unhandled shapes
cases.then: the module either handles the unscripted input sanely with a clear, documented result, or the gap is recorded as a defect — it is not silently assumed covered by the scripted cases

cases.given: SFSM-08 is evaluated from the approved Capability Registry, not from the implementation plan
cases.when: a forbidden-state oracle for visual review artifacts is checked directly, independent of any single scripted example
cases.then: a workflow/UI/FSM spec must NEVER pass validate_visual_artifact_policy when both visual_artifacts and visual_artifact_na_reason are absent, regardless of any other field

cases.given: SFSM-09 is evaluated from the approved Capability Registry, not from the implementation plan
cases.when: an unscripted exploratory charter for issue coverage artifacts: probing validate_issue_coverage with a clarification-log whose question_ids do not match the issue's referenced ids (a mismatch shape not in the scripted fixtures) to confirm it is flagged rather than accepted because *some* log exists
cases.then: the module either handles the unscripted input sanely with a clear, documented result, or the gap is recorded as a defect — it is not silently assumed covered by the scripted cases

cases.given: SFSM-09 is evaluated from the approved Capability Registry, not from the implementation plan
cases.when: a forbidden-state oracle for issue coverage artifacts is checked directly, independent of any single scripted example
cases.then: an issue whose status is needs_user must NEVER pass coverage validation regardless of any other field, asserted as an always-fails invariant rather than a single example

cases.given: SFSM-10 is evaluated from the approved Capability Registry, not from the implementation plan
cases.when: an unscripted exploratory charter for gate progress disclosure: feeding the Stop hook's progress-marker detection near-miss phrasings ("Superpower now=" missing colon, wrong case, extra whitespace) to document that the match is exact-string and near-misses are not caught
cases.then: the module either handles the unscripted input sanely with a clear, documented result, or the gap is recorded as a defect — it is not silently assumed covered by the scripted cases

cases.given: SFSM-10 is evaluated from the approved Capability Registry, not from the implementation plan
cases.when: a forbidden-state oracle for gate progress disclosure is checked directly, independent of any single scripted example
cases.then: the Stop hook must NEVER print an audit line when session.superpower_active is falsy/absent, asserted across malformed/partial session objects too, not just the one fixture already covered
